message:
perf(ready): split hybrid scan into priority buckets + use NOT NULL fast path

Two related optimizations to the `br ready` query path.

1) Hybrid sort: split into two index-friendly bucket queries

The Hybrid policy (P0/P1 first, then everything else, both
ordered by created_at) used:

  ORDER BY CASE WHEN priority <= 1 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END, created_at ASC

That CASE expression in the ORDER BY defeats every existing
single-column index — the planner has to materialize the full
candidate set into a temp B-tree to sort. On a backlog of any
size this is the bottleneck of `br ready`.

Add a `ReadyPriorityBucket::{High,Low}` enum and a new
`query_ready_hybrid_issue_candidates_with_projection()` that
runs two queries instead of one:

  Bucket High: WHERE priority <= 1 ... ORDER BY created_at ASC
  Bucket Low:  WHERE priority >  1 ... ORDER BY created_at ASC

Each subquery has a clean ORDER BY that the new
`idx_issues_ready_created` partial index can satisfy directly,
turning two index-range scans into the result set instead of a
single materialize-and-sort. Limit semantics preserved: if the
caller asked for N items, the High bucket runs with limit=N first;
if it returns fewer than N, the Low bucket runs with the remainder.

The new schema/migration adds the partial index:

  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_ready_created
    ON issues(status, created_at)
    WHERE status = 'open' AND ephemeral = 0
      AND pinned = 0 AND is_template = 0;

A new schema-presence assertion in the existing test pins it.

The bucket dispatcher is opt-in: only Hybrid + the modern
NOT-NULL flag predicate (see #2) take the split path. Priority
and Oldest sorts already had clean ORDER BY columns and stay on
the unified path; legacy schemas (where ephemeral/pinned/
is_template columns can hold NULL) also stay on the unified path
because the partial index excludes those rows.

2) Flag predicate: skip the NULL guard on schemas that disallow it

The candidate WHERE clauses had three guards:

  AND (pinned = 0 OR pinned IS NULL)
  AND (ephemeral = 0 OR ephemeral IS NULL)
  AND (is_template = 0 OR is_template IS NULL)

The OR-IS-NULL form is required for old databases whose
`ephemeral`/`pinned`/`is_template` columns can hold NULL because
they were added before defaults were enforced. On any modern
database (every one created since the NOT NULL migration) those
guards are dead weight and prevent the planner from using the
new partial index, which has `... = 0` predicates baked into its
WHERE.

Probe the schema once with PRAGMA table_info(issues) and pick:

  ReadyFlagPredicate::ExactFalse
    `pinned = 0 AND ephemeral = 0 AND is_template = 0`
    Used when all three columns are NOT NULL.

  ReadyFlagPredicate::LegacyNullAsFalse
    The old `(col = 0 OR col IS NULL)` form. Preserved for
    backwards compatibility with databases that haven't been
    migrated.

The new `ready_filter_columns_are_not_null()` helper does the
PRAGMA inspection, falling back to `LegacyNullAsFalse` on any
read error so a corrupted PRAGMA can never expose the strict path
to a database that needs the loose one.

Threaded through:
- recover_ready_issues_in_memory
- query_ready_issue_candidates_dispatch (new entry point that
  branches between hybrid-bucket and unified)
- query_ready_issue_candidates_with_projection
- build_ready_issue_candidates_query (new flag_predicate +
  priority_bucket parameters)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -90,6 +90,15 @@ pub const SCHEMA_SQL: &str = r"
         AND pinned = 0
         AND is_template = 0;
 
+    -- Hybrid ready order split path: P0/P1 by created_at, then all others
+    -- by created_at.  Keeps both bucket scans ordered without a temp sort.
+    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_ready_created
+        ON issues(status, created_at)
+        WHERE status = 'open'
+        AND ephemeral = 0
+        AND pinned = 0
+        AND is_template = 0;
+
     -- Common active list path: non-terminal issues sorted by priority/created_at.
     -- Uses ASC on created_at (not DESC) to avoid frankensqlite B-tree ordering
     -- divergence with C sqlite3 integrity_check.  SQLite reverse-scans the ASC
@@ -1270,6 +1279,13 @@ fn run_migrations(conn: &Connection, issues_rebuilt: bool) -> Result<()> {
             AND pinned = 0
             AND is_template = 0;
 
+        CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_ready_created
+            ON issues(status, created_at)
+            WHERE status = 'open'
+            AND ephemeral = 0
+            AND pinned = 0
+            AND is_template = 0;
+
         -- Common active list path: non-terminal issues sorted by priority/created_at
         CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_list_active_order
             ON issues(priority, created_at)
@@ -1906,6 +1922,10 @@ mod tests {
             indexes.contains("idx_issues_ready"),
             "missing idx_issues_ready composite index"
         );
+        assert!(
+            indexes.contains("idx_issues_ready_created"),
+            "missing idx_issues_ready_created hybrid index"
+        );
         assert!(
             indexes.contains("idx_issues_list_active_order"),
             "missing idx_issues_list_active_order composite index"
